reasoning:
Hikaru Nakamura is currently ranked #2 on the FIDE December 2025 list with a rating of 2810, 15 points ahead of #3 Fabiano Caruana (2795). Having just met the 40-game classical requirement to secure his spot in the 2026 Candidates Tournament by rating, Nakamura has massive incentive to "sit" on his 2810 rating and avoid playing classical chess. His rating will likely remain unchanged through early 2026. 

To drop to #3, Caruana (or another player like Erigaisi) would need to gain 15+ points, which is exceptionally difficult at that elite level (requiring an overwhelming tournament victory with a massive performance rating). 
- 2025-12-28: FIDE won't publish the next list until Jan 1, so the Wikipedia page will almost certainly still display the Dec 1 list, leaving Nakamura at #2. (97%)
- 2026-01-20: There are virtually no classical super-tournaments in December to allow competitors to gain 15 points. Nakamura will very likely remain #2 on the Jan 1 list. (94%)
- 2026-03-21: Incorporates Jan/Feb events like Tata Steel. Caruana could play and theoretically gain 15 points, but doing so requires an exceptionally dominant result (e.g., +4 or +5 over the field) without any upsets. Nakamura's cushion remains relatively safe. (85%)
